parameters.description:
key is a value returned in PageResponse.next_key to begin
querying the next page most efficiently. Only one of offset or key
should be set.
parameters.name: pagination.key

parameters.description:
offset is a numeric offset that can be used when key is unavailable.
It is less efficient than using key. Only one of offset or key should
be set.
parameters.name: pagination.offset

parameters.description:
limit is the total number of results to be returned in the result page.
If left empty it will default to a value to be set by each app.
parameters.name: pagination.limit

parameters.description:
count_total is set to true  to indicate that the result set should include
a count of the total number of items available for pagination in UIs.
count_total is only respected when offset is used. It is ignored when key
is set.
parameters.name: pagination.count_total

parameters.description: reverse is set to true if results are to be returned in the descending order.
parameters.name: pagination.reverse

get.summary: ListAccount Queries a list of Account items.

ListBond Queries a list of Bond items. This is what makes bonds legible:
a buyer checks a claimant's stake and the slasher lists active bonds it
is responsible for resolving.

agntcoin.agntcoin.v1.Bond.description:
Bond defines slashable collateral held by the protocol. A participant
(poster) locks `amount` behind a claim/behavior; a designated neutral
`slasher` either releases it (exonerate -> refund the poster) or slashes it
(punish -> collateral to a beneficiary, or burned). The security-critical
property — what makes it a deterrent and not a refundable deposit — is that
the poster can NEVER self-withdraw (only the slasher resolves it).
status is one of: "active" | "released" | "slashed".

slasher.title: the ONLY address that may release or slash

agntcoin.agntcoin.v1.Dispute.description:
Dispute is a k-of-n jury case opened against an escrow whose acceptability
cannot be settled by an objective test (or is contested). Eligible jurors
(the anchor set at genesis; a reputation/stake-gated registry later) cast a
Vote; once a quorum is reached the case resolves and the escrow is released
to the payee (accept) or refunded to the payer (reject).

bond_id.description:
bond_id is the slashable dispute-bond the opener posted at OpenDispute time
(purpose "dispute:<id>", ref = the contested escrow id). On ResolveDispute it
is released to the opener if their dispute is upheld, or slashed to the
griefed counterparty if the verdict goes against them (frivolous/cry-wolf).

opener.title: the payer or payee who opened the case

agntcoin.agntcoin.v1.Escrow.description:
Escrow defines a native conditional-payment record.
status is one of: "locked" | "released" | "refunded".

delivery_hash.description:
delivery_hash is a hex sha256 of the delivered artifact, committed by the
payee at submit time. It cryptographically pins the exhibit the jury judges:
anyone can re-hash the artifact a serving layer (platform) presents and check
it byte-for-byte against this on-chain commitment, so the delivery can't be
silently altered after submission — the exhibit's integrity stops resting on
trusting the platform. Empty if the payee submitted no hash.

no_auto_release.description:
no_auto_release disables the post-deadline payee-self-release path: a
jury-bound escrow can only be settled by the payer or by the jury, never by
the payee waiting out the deadline and self-paying (it13 finding #19). Set
by the payer at lock time for un-verifiable / jury-bound work. Default false
preserves the it7 anti-ghosting behavior for ordinary escrows.

agntcoin.agntcoin.v1.Params.description: Params defines the parameters for the module.

anchors.description:
anchors is the set of trust-root addresses for reputation. Reputation is a
personalized PageRank teleporting to these anchors, so a node only accrues
reputation insofar as it is reachable (via vouches or completed jobs) from
an anchor. Seeded with the founder at genesis; expandable via governance as
the trust graph matures (multi-anchor). If empty, reputation falls back to
uniform (non-personalized) PageRank.

agntcoin.agntcoin.v1.QueryParamsResponse.description: QueryParamsResponse is response type for the Query/Params RPC method.

params.description: params holds all the parameters of this module.

stake.description:
stake is the amount of ag3nt locked behind this vouch. Reputation
(PageRank) edge weights are derived from stake, making the trust graph
costly to Sybil. The stake is held (debited from the voucher) until the
vouch is removed via Unvouch.

cosmos.base.query.v1beta1.PageRequest.title:
PageRequest is to be embedded in gRPC request messages for efficient
pagination. Ex:

count_total.description:
count_total is set to true  to indicate that the result set should include
a count of the total number of items available for pagination in UIs.
count_total is only respected when offset is used. It is ignored when key
is set.

key.description:
key is a value returned in PageResponse.next_key to begin
querying the next page most efficiently. Only one of offset or key
should be set.

limit.description:
limit is the total number of results to be returned in the result page.
If left empty it will default to a value to be set by each app.

offset.description:
offset is a numeric offset that can be used when key is unavailable.
It is less efficient than using key. Only one of offset or key should
be set.

reverse.description: reverse is set to true if results are to be returned in the descending order.

next_key.description:
next_key is the key to be passed to PageRequest.key to
query the next page most efficiently. It will be empty if
there are no more results.

total.title:
total is total number of results available if PageRequest.count_total
was set, its value is undefined otherwise
